words.definitionEn: to or at the place where you live
words.exampleEn: Come on, it's time to go home.

words.definitionEn: the house or flat that you live in, especially with your family
words.exampleEn: Old people prefer to stay in their own homes.

words.definitionEn: work that is given by teachers for students to do at home
words.exampleEn: I always do my homework on the bus.

words.definitionEn: to want something to happen and think that it is possible
words.exampleEn: All we can do now is wait and hope.

words.definitionEn: a large animal with four legs, a mane (= long thick hair on its neck) and a tail. Horses are used for riding on, pulling carriages, etc.
words.exampleEn: to ride a horse

words.definitionEn: a large building where people who are ill or injured are given medical treatment and care
words.exampleEn: to/into (the) hospital, (British English), He had to go to/into hospital for treatment.

words.definitionEn: used instead of someone in negative sentences and in questions after if/whether, and after verbs such as prevent, forbid, avoid, etc.
words.exampleEn: Is anyone there?

words.definitionEn: having a high temperature; producing heat
words.exampleEn: It's hot today, isn't it?

words.definitionEn: a building where people stay, usually for a short time, paying for their rooms and meals
words.exampleEn: a two-star/five-star, etc. hotel

words.definitionEn: 60 minutes; one of the 24 parts that a day is divided into
words.exampleEn: I spent an hour on the phone.

words.definitionEn: a building for people to live in, usually for one family
words.exampleEn: in a house, We live in a two-bedroom house.

words.definitionEn: in what way or manner
words.exampleEn: How does it work?

words.definitionEn: used to introduce a statement that contrasts with something that has just been said
words.exampleEn: He was feeling bad. He went to work, however, and tried to concentrate.

words.definitionEn: used instead of something in negative sentences and in questions; after if/whether; and after verbs such as prevent, ban, avoid, etc.
words.exampleEn: Would you like anything else?
One hundred (of the children) have already been placed with foster families.

words.definitionEn: feeling that you want to eat something
words.exampleEn: I'm really hungry.

words.definitionEn: the man that somebody is married to; a married man
words.exampleEn: This is my husband, Steve.

words.definitionEn: used as the subject of a verb when the speaker or writer is referring to himself/herself
words.exampleEn: I think I'd better go now.

words.definitionEn: water that has frozen and become solid
words.exampleEn: There was ice on the windows.

words.definitionEn: a type of sweet frozen food made from milk fat, tasting of fruit, chocolate, etc. and often eaten as a dessert; a small amount of this food intended for one person, often served in a container made of biscuit that is like a cone in shape
words.exampleEn: Desserts are served with cream or ice cream.

words.definitionEn: a plan, thought or suggestion, especially about what to do in a particular situation
words.exampleEn: It would be a good idea to call before we leave.

words.definitionEn: used to say that one thing can, will or might happen or be true, depending on another thing happening or being true
words.exampleEn: If you see him, give him this note.

words.definitionEn: a set of rooms for living in, usually on one floor of a building
words.exampleEn: an apartment building

words.definitionEn: to form a picture in your mind of what something might be like
words.exampleEn: imagine something, The house was just as she had imagined it.

words.definitionEn: having a great effect on people or things; of great value
words.exampleEn: an important issue/question/point/factor

words.definitionEn: to become better than before; to make something/somebody better than before
words.exampleEn: Overall the situation has improved dramatically.

words.definitionEn: into an object, an area or a substance
words.exampleEn: She opened the door and went in.

words.definitionEn: at a point within an area or a space
words.exampleEn: a country in Africa

words.definitionEn: if one thing includes another, it has the second thing as one of its parts
words.exampleEn: include something, The tour included a visit to the Science Museum.

words.definitionEn: facts or details about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: a piece of information

words.definitionEn: a round fruit with shiny red or green skin that is fairly hard and white inside
words.exampleEn: Peel and core the apples.

words.definitionEn: the feeling that you have when you want to know or learn more about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Her research has generated interest around the world.

words.definitionEn: to attract your attention and make you feel interested; to make yourself give your attention to something
words.exampleEn: interest somebody, Politics doesn't interest me.

words.definitionEn: giving your attention to something because you enjoy finding out about it or doing it; showing interest in something and finding it exciting
words.exampleEn: He sounded genuinely interested.

words.definitionEn: attracting your attention because it is/they are special, exciting or unusual
words.exampleEn: The article raises several interesting questions.

words.definitionEn: an international computer network connecting other networks and computers that allows people to share information around the world
words.exampleEn: to surf/browse/access the internet

words.definitionEn: a formal meeting at which somebody is asked questions to see if they are suitable for a particular job, or for a course of study at a college, university, etc.
words.exampleEn: a job interview

words.definitionEn: to talk to somebody and ask them questions at a formal meeting to find out if they are suitable for a job, course of study, etc.
words.exampleEn: interview somebody, The university interviews all potential candidates.

words.definitionEn: to a position in or inside something
words.exampleEn: Come into the house.

words.definitionEn: to tell two or more people who have not met before what each other’s names are; to tell somebody what your name is
words.exampleEn: introduce somebody, Allow me to introduce my mother.

words.definitionEn: a piece of land that is completely surrounded by water
words.exampleEn: a small/tiny island

words.definitionEn: used to refer to an animal or a thing that has already been mentioned or that is being talked about now
words.exampleEn: ‘Where's your car?’ ‘It's in the garage.’

words.definitionEn: belonging to or connected with a thing, an animal or a baby
words.exampleEn: Turn the box on its side.

words.definitionEn: a piece of clothing worn on the top half of the body over a shirt, etc. that has arms and fastens down the front; a short, light coat
words.exampleEn: a leather/denim/tweed jacket

words.definitionEn: the 1st month of the year, between December and February
words.exampleEn: She was born in January.

words.definitionEn: trousers made of denim (= a type of strong cotton)
words.exampleEn: I always wear jeans.

words.definitionEn: work for which you receive regular payment
words.exampleEn: I don't have a job at present.

words.definitionEn: to fix or connect two or more things together
words.exampleEn: join A to/onto B, Join one section of pipe to the next.

words.definitionEn: an act of travelling from one place to another, especially when they are far apart
words.exampleEn: They went on a long train journey across India.

words.definitionEn: the liquid that comes from fruit or vegetables; a drink made from this
words.exampleEn: a glass of fruit juice

words.definitionEn: the 7th month of the year, between June and August
words.exampleEn: in July, She was born in July.

words.definitionEn: the 6th month of the year, between May and July
words.exampleEn: She was born in June.

words.definitionEn: only
words.exampleEn: There is just one method that might work.

words.definitionEn: to continue to have something and not give it back or throw it away
words.exampleEn: He kept all her letters.

words.definitionEn: most important; essential
words.exampleEn: the key issue/factor/point

words.definitionEn: a piece of metal with a special shape used for locking a door, starting a car, etc.
words.exampleEn: to put/turn the key in the lock

words.definitionEn: a unit for measuring distance; 1 000 metres
words.exampleEn: The industrial estate is 6 kilometres from the city centre.

words.definitionEn: a group of people or things that are the same in some way; a particular variety or type
words.exampleEn: kind of somebody/something, three kinds of cakes/cake

words.definitionEn: a room in which meals are cooked or prepared
words.exampleEn: We ate at the kitchen table.

words.definitionEn: to have information in your mind as a result of experience or because you have learned or been told it
words.exampleEn: know something, No one knows the answer.

words.definitionEn: the fourth month of the year, between March and May
words.exampleEn: in April, She was born in April.

words.definitionEn: the surface of the earth that is not sea
words.exampleEn: The new project will reclaim the land from the sea.

words.definitionEn: the system of communication in speech and writing that is used by people of a particular country or area
words.exampleEn: the English language

words.definitionEn: big in size or quantity
words.exampleEn: a large group/city/area/crowd/family

words.definitionEn: happening or coming after all other similar things or people
words.exampleEn: We caught the last bus home.

words.definitionEn: arriving, happening or done after the expected, arranged or usual time
words.exampleEn: I'm sorry I'm late.

words.definitionEn: after the expected, arranged or usual time
words.exampleEn: I got up late.

words.definitionEn: at a time in the future; after the time you are talking about
words.exampleEn: See you later.

words.definitionEn: the sound you make when you think that something is funny or silly
words.exampleEn: to give a laugh

words.definitionEn: to make the sounds and movements of your face that show you think something is funny or silly
words.exampleEn: to laugh out loud

words.definitionEn: to gain knowledge or skill by studying, from experience, from being taught, etc.
words.exampleEn: learn something, to learn a language/skill/trade

words.definitionEn: part of a place, town, etc., or a region of a country or the world
words.exampleEn: to improve access to services in rural areas

words.definitionEn: to go away from a person or a place
words.exampleEn: Come on, it's time we left (= time for us to leave).

words.definitionEn: on the side of your body that is towards the west when you are facing north
words.exampleEn: Fewer people write with their left hand than with their right.

words.definitionEn: on or to the left side
words.exampleEn: Turn left at the intersection.

words.definitionEn: the left side or direction
words.exampleEn: on the left, Take the next road on the left.

words.definitionEn: one of the long parts that connect the feet to the rest of the body
words.exampleEn: Sit on the floor, stretching your legs out in front of you.

words.definitionEn: a period of time in which somebody is taught something
words.exampleEn: She gives piano lessons.

words.definitionEn: used for making suggestions or as a polite way of telling people what you want them to do
words.exampleEn: ‘Shall we check it again?’ ‘Yes, let's.’

words.definitionEn: a written, typed or printed message that is put in an envelope or attached to an email and sent to somebody
words.exampleEn: to write/send (somebody) a letter

words.definitionEn: a building in which collections of books, newspapers, etc. and sometimes films and recorded music are kept for people to read, study or borrow
words.exampleEn: a public/university/school library

words.definitionEn: to be or put yourself in a flat position so that you are not standing or sitting
words.exampleEn: lie + adv./prep., to lie on your back/side/front

words.definitionEn: the ability to breathe, grow, produce young, etc. that people, animals and plants have before they die and that objects do not have
words.exampleEn: This could mean the difference between life and death.

words.definitionEn: pale in colour
words.exampleEn: He's got light blue eyes.

words.definitionEn: the energy from the sun, a lamp, etc. that makes it possible to see things
words.exampleEn: bright/dim light

words.definitionEn: either of the two long parts that stick out from the top of the body and connect the shoulders to the hands
words.exampleEn: He escaped with only a broken arm.

words.definitionEn: similar to somebody/something
words.exampleEn: She's wearing a dress like mine.

words.definitionEn: to find somebody/something pleasant, attractive or of a good enough standard; to enjoy something
words.exampleEn: like somebody/something, She's nice. I like her.

words.definitionEn: a long, thin mark on a surface
words.exampleEn: a straight/solid/dotted/dashed line

words.definitionEn: a large, powerful animal of the cat family that hunts in groups and lives in parts of Africa and southern Asia. Lions have yellow-brown fur and the male has a mane (= long, thick hair round its neck).
words.exampleEn: The lion shook its mane and roared.

words.definitionEn: a series of names, items, figures, etc., especially when they are written or printed
words.exampleEn: The guest list includes numerous celebrities.

words.definitionEn: to write a list of things in a particular order
words.exampleEn: We were asked to list our ten favourite songs.

words.definitionEn: to pay attention to somebody/something that you can hear
words.exampleEn: Listen! What's that noise? Can you hear it?

words.definitionEn: not big; small; smaller than others
words.exampleEn: a little house

words.definitionEn: used with uncountable nouns to mean ‘a small amount’, ‘some’
words.exampleEn: a little milk/sugar/tea

words.definitionEn: used with uncountable nouns to mean ‘a small amount’, ‘some’
words.exampleEn: a little milk/sugar/tea

words.definitionEn: to have your home in a particular place
words.exampleEn: to live in a house/a flat/an apartment

words.definitionEn: belonging to or connected with the particular place or area that you are talking about or with the place where you live
words.exampleEn: local people/residents/businesses

words.definitionEn: approximately
words.exampleEn: He arrived around five o'clock.

words.definitionEn: surrounding somebody/something; on each side of something
words.exampleEn: The house is built around a central courtyard.

words.definitionEn: measuring or covering a great length or distance, or a greater length or distance than usual
words.exampleEn: She had long dark hair.

words.definitionEn: for a long time
words.exampleEn: Have you been here long?

words.definitionEn: to turn your eyes in a particular direction
words.exampleEn: Look closely and tell me what you see.

words.definitionEn: to be unable to find something/somebody
words.exampleEn: I've lost my keys.

words.definitionEn: used with verbs to mean ‘a great amount’
words.exampleEn: I care a lot about you.

words.definitionEn: a large number or amount of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: What a lot of presents!

words.definitionEn: a large number or amount
words.exampleEn: ‘How many do you need?’ ‘A lot.’

words.definitionEn: a very strong feeling of liking and caring for somebody/something, especially a member of your family or a friend
words.exampleEn: She has earned the love and respect of many people.

words.definitionEn: to have very strong feelings of liking and caring for somebody
words.exampleEn: I love you.

words.definitionEn: a meal eaten in the middle of the day
words.exampleEn: She's gone to lunch.

words.definitionEn: a piece of equipment with many parts that work together to do a particular task. The power used to work a machine may be electricity, steam, gas, etc. or human power.
words.exampleEn: Machines have replaced human labour in many industries.

words.definitionEn: a type of large thin book with a paper cover that you can buy every week or month, containing articles, photographs, etc., often on a particular topic; a similar collection of articles, etc. that appears regularly online
words.exampleEn: a weekly/monthly magazine

words.definitionEn: being the largest or most important of its kind
words.exampleEn: Be careful crossing the main road.

words.definitionEn: to create or prepare something by combining materials or putting parts together
words.exampleEn: make something, to make a table/dress/cake

words.definitionEn: to get to a place, especially at the end of a journey
words.exampleEn: I'll wait until they arrive.

words.definitionEn: an adult male human
words.exampleEn: a good-looking young man

words.definitionEn: used with plural nouns and verbs, especially in negative sentences or in more formal English, to mean ‘a large number of’. Also used in questions to ask about the size of a number, and with ‘as’, ‘so’ and ‘too’.
words.exampleEn: We don't have very many copies left.

words.definitionEn: used with plural nouns and verbs, especially in negative sentences or in more formal English, to mean ‘a large number of’. Also used in questions to ask about the size of a number, and with ‘as’, ‘so’ and ‘too’.
words.exampleEn: We don't have very many copies left.

words.definitionEn: a drawing or plan of the earth’s surface or part of it, showing countries, towns, rivers, etc.
words.exampleEn: a map of France

words.definitionEn: the 3rd month of the year, between February and April
words.exampleEn: She was born in March.

words.definitionEn: the use of the imagination to express ideas or feelings, particularly in painting, drawing or sculpture
words.exampleEn: modern/contemporary art

words.definitionEn: an occasion when people buy and sell goods; the open area or building where they meet to do this
words.exampleEn: a fruit/flower/fish market

words.definitionEn: a piece of writing about a particular subject in a newspaper or magazine, on a website, etc.
words.exampleEn: to read/write/publish an article

words.definitionEn: having a husband or wife
words.exampleEn: a married man/woman

words.definitionEn: a sports event where people or teams compete against each other
words.exampleEn: (British English), a football match

words.definitionEn: to find somebody/something that goes together with or is connected with another person or thing
words.exampleEn: match A and B, Match the words and pictures.

words.definitionEn: the fifth month of the year, between April and June
words.exampleEn: She was born in May.

words.definitionEn: a person who creates works of art, especially paintings or drawings
words.exampleEn: an exhibition of work by contemporary British artists

words.definitionEn: used when you are not certain that something will happen or that something is true or is a correct number
words.exampleEn: Maybe he'll come, maybe he won't.

words.definitionEn: the form of I that is used when the speaker or writer is the object of a verb or preposition, or after the verb be
words.exampleEn: Don't hit me.

words.definitionEn: an occasion when people eat food, especially breakfast, lunch or dinner
words.exampleEn: Try not to eat between meals.

words.definitionEn: to have something as a meaning in the same or another language
words.exampleEn: mean something, What does this sentence mean?

words.definitionEn: the thing or idea that a sound, word, sign, etc. represents
words.exampleEn: What's the meaning of this word?

words.definitionEn: the soft part of an animal or a bird that can be eaten as food; a particular type of this
words.exampleEn: a piece/slice of meat

words.definitionEn: to be in the same place as somebody by chance and talk to them
words.exampleEn: Maybe we'll meet again some time.
